verdict: All checks pass. The reading is well-structured, within word-count and time targets, properly cites all three resources, stays within scope, and meets beginner-readability standards.

format_discipline.notes: No frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and valid. Exactly six sections in correct order.

length.notes: 1389 words is within 1200–1600 target. 1389 / 230 wpm = 6.0 min, within 6–8 min range.

citations.notes: [1] (arxiv.org, primary) cited at lines 27, 31, 116, 121, 129. [2] cited at lines 11, 48, 54, 58, 64, 114, 115, 122, 123, 124, 131. [3] cited at lines 11, 117, 122, 125, 133. References section lists all three, no orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: All terms used freely (LLM, token, autoregressive decoding) are established in prior topics 3.2 and 3.4 per corpus prerequisites. New terms for this topic (factual hallucination, attributional hallucination, faithful-but-wrong hallucination, RLHF, overconfidence bias, grounding, provenance) are all defined on first use. RAG is named and deferred explicitly. Topic 3.8 (calibration) is a prior topic, so the table reference to it is not a forward reference.

faithfulness.notes: All 12 sampled assertions traced to corpus sections 5.1–5.5 and section 6. The worked example (Sweller/Kalyuga psychology citation) is a legitimate illustrative scenario rather than a factual assertion; it is not sourced from the corpus but it makes no independent factual claims about the real world.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined at first use. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously. Concepts introduced with concrete examples/analogies before abstraction (Paris/France analogy, worked example precedes abstraction). No hedging phrases found. Average sentence length is well under 25 words.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences; most are 3–4 sentences. Five-step process rendered as numbered list. Do/Don't rendered as bullet lists. Tables used for structured comparisons. Bold-term definitions present throughout (Training data, RLHF, overconfidence bias, Grounding, RAG, Provenance).

assessment_reference.notes: No mention of assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading covers definition, three hallucination types, structural mechanism (token prediction / plausibility vs truth), training data gaps, RLHF overconfidence bias, and mitigation strategies. RAG is named and deferred in one sentence with explicit 'covered in a later topic' pointer. Fine-tuning and prompt injection are not introduced. Content does not exceed the topic headline.

gold_example_comparison: No gold example file found at .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/ for this topic; comparison not applicable.
